title: [bug] Level number digits at x=289/311 instead of right-anchored at absolute x=544
description:
Audit pass 2 (m-2026-05-02-019) finding P5. Confirmed by gjm pass-2 spec review pre-location.

## Original
original/level.c:210 — level number digits drawn right-anchored at x=260 within levelWindow (absolute x=544 in mainWindow). Digit size 30x40, stride 32 (matching score digit conventions).

## Modern
src/game_render.c:450-458 — level digits at x=289 (LEVEL_AREA_X) or x=311. Different anchor and likely different digit dimensions.

## Fix scope
src/game_render.c game_render_lives — render level number digits right-anchored at LEVEL_AREA_X + 260, with 30x40 digit size and 32px stride. Use the same digit-rendering helper as score (DrawOutNumber-equivalent) to maintain consistency. Pure-logic test for position math.
acceptance_criteria: Level number digits render right-anchored at the original position. Side-by-side with screenshots/xb-game.gif shows position parity.
close_reason: Implemented in basket 4: lives now right-anchored at LEVEL_AREA_X+175 stride 30, level number right-anchored at LEVEL_AREA_X+260 stride 32. Pure-logic helpers in include/game_render.h, 10 cmocka cases in test_game_render_geometry.c.

title: [bug] Lives display: left-anchored at x=289+i*18 instead of right-anchored at x=175 stride=30
description:
Audit pass 2 (m-2026-05-02-019) finding P4. Confirmed by gjm pass-2 spec review (m-2026-05-02-018) pre-location.

## Original
original/level.c:224 — lives display draws right-to-left, anchored at x=175 within levelWindow, stride=30 between lives.

## Modern
src/game_render.c:435 — game_render_lives draws left-to-right, anchored at x=289+i*18, stride=18 between lives.

## Player impact
Lives icons appear in wrong position relative to original level info panel layout. With other panel issues (level number position), the whole level info area looks misaligned to a 1995 player.

## Fix scope
src/game_render.c game_render_lives — change to right-anchored: bullet i at x = LEVEL_AREA_X + 175 - i*30 (verify against original/level.c:224 absolute coordinate). Stride 30 not 18. Add characterization test for the position math (pure-logic, no SDL needed).
acceptance_criteria: Lives icons render right-to-left from absolute x=464 (LEVEL_AREA_X=289 + 175) with 30px stride. Position matches original level info panel.
close_reason: Implemented in basket 4: lives now right-anchored at LEVEL_AREA_X+175 stride 30, level number right-anchored at LEVEL_AREA_X+260 stride 32. Pure-logic helpers in include/game_render.h, 10 cmocka cases in test_game_render_geometry.c.

title: [bug] Ball launch guide oscillates 2.67x too fast — wrong frame-rate multiplier
description:
Audit pass 1 (m-2026-05-02-017) finding P3.

## Original
original/ball.c:456 — guide advances every frame % (BALL_FRAME_RATE * 8) frames. BALL_FRAME_RATE = 5, so guide steps every 40 frames.

## Modern
src/ball_system.c:918 — guide advances every frame % (BALL_FRAME_RATE * 3) = every 15 frames. 2.67x faster than original.

## Player impact
Affects every ball launch. Player waiting to launch sees the direction guide sweeping too fast — harder to aim.

## Fix scope
src/ball_system.c:918 — change BALL_FRAME_RATE * 3 to BALL_FRAME_RATE * 8. One-line change. Add characterization test asserting guide step rate.
acceptance_criteria: Guide oscillation matches original — visibly slower sweep. Ball launch aiming feels deliberate, not frantic.

title: [bug] DROP_BLK wrong sprite (red instead of green) and missing hit-count digit overlay
description:
Audit pass 1 (m-2026-05-02-017) finding P2.

## Original
original/blocks.c:1727-1735 — DROP_BLK renders the green block pixmap (greenblock), then overlays a centered text string of the decimal hit-point count (blockP->hitPoints) using dataFont in black.

## Modern
src/sprite_catalog.h:338-339 — sprite_block_key(DROP_BLK) returns SPR_BLOCK_RED. No text overlay. game_render_blocks (src/game_render.c:70-120) draws one texture from the catalog and stops; there is no per-block composite rendering path.

## Two defects
1. Wrong base sprite: green should be used, not red
2. Missing hit-count digit overlay

## Fix scope
Return SPR_BLOCK_GREEN for DROP_BLK in sprite_block_key. Add composite text rendering path in game_render_blocks for DROP_BLK that overlays the hit-points digit using SDL2F_FONT_DATA (or equivalent SDL2 font slot) in black. Compositing infrastructure may be reusable for DROP_BLK and RANDOM_BLK + future RANDOM/DROP-style overlays.
acceptance_criteria: DROP_BLK appears as a green block with its hit-point count digit visible in black. Matches original/blocks.c:1727 visual.
close_reason: Basket 2 fix landed in branch fix/visual-basket2-block-composites (PR pending). DROP→green+digit, RANDOM→red+text, BULLET→yellow+4 bullets composited.

title: [bug] BONUSX2/X4/BONUS_BLK never animate — frozen at frame 1 instead of 4-frame spin cycle
description:
Audit pass 1 (m-2026-05-02-017) finding P1.

## Original
original/blocks.c:1763-1773 — BONUSX2/BONUSX4/BONUS_BLK each call RenderShape with a slide index into a 4-frame array (x2bonus[slide], x4bonus[slide], Bonus[slide]). bonusSlide cycles 3→2→1→0 via HandlePendingBonuses() at original/blocks.c:1194-1217.

src/sprite_catalog.h:350-355 — sprite_block_key returns frame-1 keys only (SPR_BLOCK_X2_1, SPR_BLOCK_X4_1, SPR_BLOCK_BONUS_1). game_render_blocks (src/game_render.c:100) calls sprite_block_key with no animation frame. block_system_render_info_t exposes bonus_slide (src/block_system.c:813) but the renderer ignores it for these types.

## Fix scope
Add animation frame selection in game_render_blocks() for BONUSX2_BLK, BONUSX4_BLK, BONUS_BLK using info.bonus_slide. Either expand sprite_block_key signature to accept a frame index, or add type-specific switch in game_render_blocks.
acceptance_criteria: BONUS-family blocks animate through 4-frame spin cycle. Visible in any level containing these blocks. Side-by-side with screenshots/xb-blocks.gif.
close_reason: Basket 1 fix landed in branch feat/visual-fidelity-audit (PR pending). sprite_block_animated_key sibling helper + game_render_blocks integration + 11 cmocka tests.

title: [epic] Visual fidelity audit — comprehensive comparison of modern build vs 1996 original screenshots
description:
Maintainer dogfood (2026-05-02) surfaced multiple visual gaps in the modern build vs the 1996 original. Two specific bugs already filed (xboing-c-tmr block explosion animation; xboing-c-fuz bullet counter misalignment) but the maintainer reports 'many more' visual issues. This epic commissions a comprehensive audit covering every visible element.

## Scope of audit

Every visual element in the play loop and surrounding screens. For each, the audit must document the canonical 1996 behavior (with original/<file>.c:<line> citations) + the modern current state (with src/<file>.c:<line> citations) + a gap verdict + minimal fix scope.

Areas to cover:

1. **Block rendering** — sprite frames, KILL_BLK explosion animation, COUNTER_BLK countdown digit, animated blocks (bomb fuse, hyperspace shimmer, etc.)
2. **Level info panel (top)** — bombs row, bullets/ammo row, level number digits, score display geometry
3. **Score / lives / timer** — font, digit sprite vs ttf, position, color, blink/highlight states
4. **Specials panel (bottom)** — verify all 8 labels render correctly (Reverse, Save, NoWall, x2, Sticky, FastGun, Killer, x4); position; active-yellow vs inactive-white; shadow text
5. **Bullet rendering** — in-flight sprite + ammo belt sprite; tinks
6. **Ball rendering** — colors, trail, speed-tier visuals
7. **Paddle** — size sprites (40/50/70), animation, sticky highlight, reverse-mode visual
8. **Eyedude** — animation, position, fire reaction, death
9. **Background / border** — play area framing, level backdrop tile, message strip
10. **Particle effects** — bullet tinks, block debris, paddle hit feedback
11. **Bonus screen** — layout, animation, score breakdown
12. **Pause overlay** — text, position
13. **Game over** — animation, score display
14. **Attract / intro / presents / keys / highscore screens** — visual fidelity
15. **Window geometry** — main window dimensions, sub-window positions; aspect/scaling

## Reference materials

Original screenshots in screenshots/:
- xb-game.gif (gameplay)
- xb-blocks.gif (block types)
- xb-demo.gif (attract mode)
- xb-editor.gif (level editor)
- xb-inst.gif (instructions)
- xb-keys.gif (keys screen)
- xb-prev.gif (level preview)
- xb-scores.gif (high scores)

Modern screenshot: ~/Pictures/Screenshot from 2026-05-01 21-02-59.png (post-PR #101 dogfood by maintainer)

## Resolution

Research mission produces docs/research/ artifact with gap inventory + fix scope per area. Fix mission(s) close the gaps in a single PR per the maintainer's stated preference. Children beads filed if any gap warrants its own dedicated tracking.
acceptance_criteria: Modern build's visual layout, animations, and sprite usage match the 1996 original's intent across all visible elements. Maintainer dogfood confirms parity. Side-by-side comparison with screenshots/xb-*.gif shows no obvious discrepancies.

title: [bug] Block explosion animation missing — blocks vanish instantly instead of cycling KILL_BLK frames
description:
Audit pass 2 (m-2026-05-02-019) finding P1 — STRUCTURAL gap confirmed.

## Symptom
Maintainer dogfood (2026-05-02): blocks vanish instantly when destroyed. The original showed an explosion/debris animation across multiple KILL_BLK frames before the block was cleared.

## Structural absence — explosion infrastructure scaffolded but unused

- include/block_system.h:56 — BLOCK_EXPLODE_DELAY=10 defined but never referenced anywhere
- src/block_system.c:32-35 — explosion state-machine fields exist (exploding, explode_start_frame, explode_next_frame, explode_slide) but are NEVER WRITTEN after initialization
- src/game_callbacks.c:90 — calls block_system_clear() directly on ball collision, bypassing any explosion lifecycle
- ExplodeBlocksPending() (original/blocks.c) has no equivalent in modern code

## Original behavior
On block hit, original sets blockP->exploding=True via SetBlockUpForExplosion. ExplodeBlocksPending advances explode_slide per BLOCK_EXPLODE_DELAY ticks, rendering KILL_BLK sprite at the current frame index. When slide reaches the end, the cell is cleared.

## Fix scope
- include/block_system.h: expose block_system_explode(ctx, row, col) public API
- src/block_system.c: implement the state machine — set exploding=1, advance per BLOCK_EXPLODE_DELAY in block_system_update, render KILL_BLK frame from explode_slide
- src/game_callbacks.c:90: replace direct block_system_clear with block_system_explode (or trigger via callback)
- src/game_render.c: render KILL_BLK sprite from explode_slide while block is in exploding state
- Test: state machine transitions (exploding flag, slide advancement, BLOCK_EXPLODE_DELAY consumption); visual eyeball

## Acceptance
Blocks visibly explode through KILL_BLK animation frames before disappearing. Frame count and tick rate match original. block_system_clear is not called from game_callbacks.c on bullet/ball block hits — explosion path is used instead.
acceptance_criteria: Blocks cycle through KILL_BLK animation when destroyed. Visible debris/explosion before the cell becomes empty. Matches original/screenshots/xb-blocks.gif visual cadence.
close_reason: Implemented in PR (basket 3 of visual-fidelity audit). Block explosion lifecycle: block_system_explode + block_system_update_explosions state machine; hit-time vs finalize-time side-effect split; render path stage-4 skip + slide-1 offset; 14 cmocka tests; make check + dogfood pass.

title: [epic] Gun feature integration audit — modern build is missing most of the original gun feature
description:
The dogfood that revealed xboing-jm8 also surfaced a much larger gap: the gun feature was never fully integrated from the original. Beyond the 1-line gating fix that landed in PR #100, the modern build is missing audio, UI, and most pickup handlers that the 1996 game shipped with.

## Known gaps observed by maintainer (2026-05-01 dogfood)

1. **No bullet counter UI** — original drew an ammo belt via ReDrawBulletsLeft() at original/level.c:324 (bullet sprites in levelWindow at y=43). Modern src/game_render.c game_render_bullets only renders in-flight bullets; there is no visual indicator of remaining ammo.

2. **No audio on MGUN_BLK pickup** — original/blocks.c:780 played 'ammo' sound at volume 70. Modern src/game_callbacks.c:139-143 sets the unlimited+FAST_GUN flags silently.

3. **BULLET_BLK falls through default case** — original/blocks.c:776 played 'ammo' at volume 30 and granted ammo via IncBullets. Modern src/game_callbacks.c block-hit switch has no BULLET_BLK case — pickup just clears the block, no ammo gain, no audio.

4. **MAXAMMO_BLK falls through default case** — same gap as BULLET_BLK. Original granted max ammo. Modern silently clears.

5. **Fire key may not be canonical** — current modern binding is K per src/game_input.c:297. The 1996 original may have used a different key (likely 'B' for bullet, or space, or arrow — unverified). Maintainer flagged this as suspicious.

## Investigation scope (to be filled by research mission)

- Fire key binding: read original/keys.c, original/keysedit.c, original/main.c
- All gun-related blocks: BULLET_BLK, MAXAMMO_BLK, MGUN_BLK behaviors (pickup effect, audio cue, score, animation)
- Audio cues: pickup, fire, click-when-empty, ricochet, etc.
- UI: ammo belt geometry, sprite, location, redraw triggers
- Bullet rendering: in-flight (modern has) + counter (modern lacks)
- Lifecycle: ammo persistence across level transitions, ball deaths, game over
- Dual-fire with FAST_GUN: how it interacts with single-fire mode
- Limits: MAX_BULLETS, MAX_MOVING_BULLETS, GUN_AMMO_PER_LEVEL canonical value

## Resolution

Research mission produces docs/research/ artifact. Fix mission(s) close the integration gaps in one or more PRs. Children beads filed as research findings firm up.

## Context

xboing-jm8 (gun firing gating) was closed in PR #100 with a 1-line fix to gun_system_shoot's predicate. That covered the firing-after-pickup symptom but NOT the broader feature integration. This epic captures the rest.
acceptance_criteria: Modern build matches original gun feature behavior — verified by maintainer playthrough. All gun-related blocks grant their canonical effect with audio cue. Bullet counter UI present and updates correctly. Fire key matches original binding. Characterization tests cover the gun lifecycle.
close_reason: All 13 gun feature gaps fixed in branch feat/gun-feature-integration (PR pending)

title: [bug] Ball passes through blocks without breaking them (collision tunneling or grid miss)
description:
Player observed ball traveling through 2 rows of blocks in level 1 without registering collision or breaking blocks (2026-05-01 dogfood).

## Symptom

In one observed instance, the ball moved through 2 rows of blocks in a single trajectory without collision detection firing — no blocks broken, no bounce, ball passed through as if the blocks weren't there.

## Hypotheses

1. **Tunneling at high speed**: ball traveled more than one block-cell width in a single physics tick — collision check ran from old position to new position via discrete sampling and skipped the blocks between. The original used continuous swept-collision; modern code may have regressed to discrete.
2. **Wrong block grid queried**: a stale or empty block grid was checked due to a state-management bug.
3. **Region math edge case**: blocks at certain row/col combinations have wrong region geometry (e.g., off-by-one in region bounds), causing the ball's region intersection to miss them.

## Investigate

Read-set:
- original/ball.c (collision detection, particularly the swept-line / multi-step routine — likely BounceBall or similar)
- original/blocks.c (block region geometry, collision response)
- src/ball_system.c (current ball physics + collision math)
- src/block_system.c (current block grid queries)
- include/ball_system.h, include/block_system.h
- ball_math.c (collision geometry primitives — referenced in cppcheck variableScope:ball_math.c suppression)
- tests/test_ball_*.c (existing ball-collision characterization tests)

## Resolution

Identify root cause; reproduce in a unit test if possible (deterministic ball trajectory through known block grid); fix without changing legacy collision feel.

title: Specials panel not rendered — no visual indicator for active powerups
description:
Specials panel (8 powerup labels at screen x=292 y=655 w=180 h=35) is never drawn in the integration build, despite the rendering API being fully designed for it.

## State of play

- include/special_system.h:155-159 exposes special_system_get_labels(ctx, reverse_on, out) which returns 8 special_label_info_t (label text, col_x, row, active flag) — explicit comment says 'Layout matches legacy DrawSpecials()'
- include/special_system.h:25-33 defines panel layout constants (SPECIAL_PANEL_W=180, SPECIAL_PANEL_H=35, four column offsets, two row offsets, GAP)
- src/game_render.c lines 5 and 53 document the specials panel in comments but contain no draw call — searching for 'special' in game_render.c returns only those two comment lines
- Canonical reference: original/special.c:150 — DrawSpecials(Display *display) — defines the legacy layout, fonts, and active/inactive colors

## Fix shape

Single integration wiring: add a render call in src/game_render.c that invokes special_system_get_labels(), iterates the 8 entries, and draws each at (panel_x + col_x, panel_y + row*(text_h + SPECIAL_GAP)) with active/inactive coloring matching original/special.c:150.

No module code changes — special_system, paddle_system (for reverse_on), and the existing text-rendering helpers in game_render.c already exist.

title: [bug] Bonus screen: coin-by-coin BONUS_BLK animation absent — modern shows static count string
description:
Audit pass 2 (m-2026-05-02-019) finding P10.

## Original
original/bonus.c:280-389 — DoBonuses() draws one BONUS_BLK sprite per call at incrementally wider position, decrementing numBonus, scoring one coin's value (3000), playing 'bonus' sound. One coin per LINE_DELAY interval. DoBullets() at 431-490 does the same for ammo: one bullet sprite per tick with score increment.

## Modern
src/game_render_ui.c:538-542 — shows a static count string 'Bonus Coins: N x 3000 = M'. No animated coin sprites. No incremental scoring. No sound per coin.

## Fix scope
src/game_render_ui.c game_render_bonus — replace the static count string with a coin-by-coin animation loop driven by the bonus state machine. One coin sprite drawn per LINE_DELAY tick at accumulating x. Each coin increments score and plays 'bonus' sound. Same pattern for bullets in BONUS_BULLET state.
acceptance_criteria: During bonus screen, coins appear one at a time accumulating across the screen with score and sound. Bullets similarly. Matches original's DoBonuses + DoBullets.

title: [bug] Bonus screen renders all states simultaneously instead of line-by-line at LINE_DELAY=100
description:
Audit pass 2 (m-2026-05-02-019) finding P6.

## Original
original/bonus.c:270-602 — state machine: BONUS_SCORE → BONUS_BONUS → BONUS_LEVEL → BONUS_BULLET → BONUS_TIME → BONUS_HSCORE → BONUS_END_TEXT → BONUS_FINISH. Each state advance gated by LINE_DELAY=100 frames between lines. Lines appear one-by-one, accumulating ypos down the screen.

## Modern
src/game_render_ui.c:532-567 — game_render_bonus renders all states visible simultaneously based on state >= BONUS_STATE_* threshold. Each state advances on time but lines appear all-at-once once the threshold is reached.

## Fix scope
Modify the bonus screen render loop to drive line reveal with per-state frame + LINE_DELAY delay (matching original's 100-frame cadence). Use ypos accumulation. State machine test on reveal timing.
acceptance_criteria: Bonus screen lines appear one at a time at LINE_DELAY=100 frame intervals. Player sees the score breakdown reveal sequentially as in original.

title: [bug] Playfield border doesn't change to green in NoWalls mode
description:

## Original

## Modern

acceptance_criteria: When player picks up WALLOFF_BLK, the play area border changes from red to green. When walls are restored (level transition or reverse pickup), border returns to red.

title: [bug] BULLET_BLK shows MAXAMMO sprite — visually conflated with unlimited-ammo block
description:
Audit pass 1 (m-2026-05-02-017) finding P6.

## Original
original/blocks.c:1680-1686 — BULLET_BLK renders a yellow block, then composites 4 bullet sprites at offsets (6,10), (15,10), (24,10), (33,10). Distinct from MAXAMMO_BLK which uses dedicated lotsammo.xpm pixmap.

Two options:
A. Composite path: render SPR_BLOCK_YELLOW + 4 SPR_BULLET overlays in game_render_blocks for BULLET_BLK
B. Dedicated asset: pre-composite a lotsammo-style PNG showing yellow + 4 bullets, add a new SPR_BLOCK_BULLET_AMMO key

Option A is more faithful to the original (compositing was per-frame). Option B is simpler if no other compositing infrastructure is needed.

title: [bug] Bullet counter misaligned — sprites appear clipped/wrong position vs original
description:
Maintainer dogfood (2026-05-02): the ammo belt rendered by game_render_ammo_belt looks wrong. Sprites appear as tiny marks near the top of the level panel rather than matching the original's clean horizontal strip below the bomb row.

## Symptom

Compare ~/Pictures/Screenshot from 2026-05-01 21-02-59.png (modern, post-PR #101) to screenshots/xb-game.gif (original). In the original the bullet strip is a clear horizontal row of bullet sprites in the level info panel, sized clearly visible to the player. In the modern build the strip appears small and possibly clipped/overlapping with the lives or level-number digits.

## Possible causes

1. Y position wrong — LEVEL_AREA_Y + 43 = y=48 may overlap or be offset from the original's y position relative to the actual level-window geometry
2. X formula correct but rendering above/below the visible level area
3. SPR_BULLET texture is the in-flight bullet sprite (7x16) but the original used a different sprite for the ammo-belt indicator
4. Lives + level digits + ammo belt all share the same Y row and overlap

## Investigate

Read-set:
- src/game_render.c game_render_lives (current life ball + level digit + ammo belt rendering — all in same function)
- src/game_render.c game_render_ammo_belt (added in PR #101)
- include/game_render.h (LEVEL_AREA_X / LEVEL_AREA_Y constants)
- original/level.c:213-334 (DisplayLevelInfo + ReDrawBulletsLeft + AddABullet)
- original/init.c (levelWindow geometry)
- bitmaps/guns/bullet.xpm (in-flight bullet) and any ammo-belt-specific bullet sprite (small marker variant?)

## Resolution

Either fix Y/X to match original's strip position, OR add a separate sprite key for the ammo-belt indicator if the original used a smaller marker sprite. Visual diff against xb-game.gif on the same level should match.
acceptance_criteria: Bullet counter displays as a clean horizontal strip in the level info panel matching the original's visual layout. Maintainer can count remaining ammo at a glance during gameplay.
close_reason: Subsumed by xboing-c-y7s pass-2 P4 (lives anchor) and P5 (level number position). The bullet strip's perceived misalignment is a downstream effect of the level info panel layout being wrong. Fixing the lives + level number anchors will correctly position the bullet strip relative to those elements. Tracked under xboing-c-y7s epic.

title: [bug] Skull (death) block collision does not fire on ball impact
description:
Player ball collided with skull blocks twice during level 1 (2026-05-01 dogfood); no death-block effect fired — no death animation, no ball loss, no points awarded.

## Symptom

Maintainer's ball touched skull blocks (BLK_DEATH or similar) twice during level 1 gameplay. Expected: ball dies, level decrements ball count, respawn. Observed: nothing happens, ball passes through or bounces normally.

## Investigate

Read-set:
- original/blocks.c (death/skull block hit handling — likely BLK_DEATH branch)
- original/ball.c (ball-block collision dispatch + DoStuffOnBlock or equivalent)
- src/block_system.c (current block collision/effect logic)
- src/ball_system.c (current ball-block collision dispatch)
- include/block_system.h, include/ball_system.h
- src/game_callbacks.c (death callbacks)

## Hypotheses

1. Skull block type isn't dispatched in the modern collision handler (BLK_DEATH case missing or returns 0)
2. Block-type registration in level loader is wrong; skulls load as a different block type
3. Death callback isn't wired in game_init.c

## Resolution

Skull block collision fires per legacy semantics (ball death + respawn). Add characterization test for the death-block collision dispatch.

title: Extend jck role with Write+Bash for docs/ paths so research/design missions can self-file
description:
Workers in ethos missions cannot reliably file their own runtime result artifacts via 'ethos mission result' — discovered across two consecutive missions (m-2026-05-02-002 jck research; m-2026-05-02-003 gjm spec-review). Two distinct failure modes:

1. **Read-only role gap (jck)**: jck's role explicitly excludes Write and Bash (Read/Grep/Glob/WebFetch only per CLAUDE.md). He cannot write to his write_set OR run 'ethos mission result'. The COO must scribe both the docs/research file content AND the runtime result.

2. **Session/turn limit gap (gjm)**: gjm has Write+Bash by role, successfully wrote docs/reviews/2026-05-01-specials-fix-spec-review.md, but ended his session before running 'ethos mission result'. The COO had to scribe the runtime result.

Both break the worker-files-through-runtime trust boundary and add a scribe hop that scales poorly. Two fix options:

A. Extend read-only roles (jck) with Write+Bash scoped to docs/research/, docs/specs/, docs/reviews/ — addresses gap 1.

B. Make 'ethos mission result' filing the FIRST action of the worker prompt rather than the last — file an empty/skeleton result on entry, append/finalize at exit. Addresses gap 2.

Both are needed for full autonomy. Verify by re-running a research and a review mission and confirming both workers self-file results without scribe assistance.

title: Distribute xboing via Snap Store (edge → stable)
description:
Package xboing as a Snap and ship it through the Snap Store as the primary Linux distribution channel. The .deb stays available for users who prefer it. One binary serves both via a small runtime path resolver — no Snap-specific code paths beyond a single env-var check.

## Why
Snap is the lowest-friction way for Ubuntu / cross-distro Linux users to install xboing with auto-update and a sandboxed runtime. Strict confinement + standard plugs means automated review (no human gate, minutes-to-publish). Snap is primary; .deb is the alternate channel.

## Design — single binary, both channels

### Read-only assets (levels, sounds, sprites)
Search list, first hit wins:
1. $XBOING_LEVELS_DIR / $XBOING_SOUND_DIR (existing env overrides)
2. $SNAP/usr/share/xboing/<subdir> when $SNAP is set (snapd injects $SNAP inside confinement; testing for it is the entire snap-detection surface)
3. $XDG_DATA_DIRS walked with xboing/<subdir> appended — covers /usr/share/xboing (.deb), /usr/local/share/xboing (source install)
4. Compile-time LEVEL_INSTALL_DIR / SOUNDS_DIR fallback (set by -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr)

No snap layouts in snapcraft.yaml — layouts are the adapter for pre-compiled third-party binaries that expect FHS paths, not for our own code.

### Writable user data (high scores, saves, config)
Use SDL_GetPrefPath("", "xboing") — returns ~/.local/share/xboing/ outside snap, ~/snap/xboing/<rev>/.local/share/xboing/ inside (snapd auto-remaps $HOME). snapd backs the snap path up across refreshes (SNAP_USER_DATA semantics). One call, both contexts. No org nesting (this is a personal project).

This replaces the legacy compile-time HIGH_SCORE_FILE path that lands in /var/games/ or similar — broken under snap confinement and bad practice on modern Linux anyway.

## Scope

### In (this bead)
- Path resolver utility: src/path_resolve.c implementing the search list above; one function per resource type or one generic function with a category arg
- Migrate level_load, sound load, sprite load call sites to use the resolver
- Migrate HIGH_SCORE_FILE writes/reads to SDL_GetPrefPath
- snapcraft.yaml at repo root targeting core24 (fall back to core22 if core24 lacks an SDL2 stack we need)
- build-packages: libsdl2-dev, libsdl2-image-dev, libsdl2-mixer-dev, libsdl2-ttf-dev
- stage-packages bundling SDL2 runtime libs
- plugs: opengl, audio-playback, home, network, joystick
- Build via Launchpad: snapcraft remote-build --target-arch=amd64,arm64
- Register name: snapcraft register xboing
- Upload + edge release
- Verify edge install on clean Ubuntu 22.04 + 24.04
- Store listing on snapcraft.io: screenshots, description, icon
- Promote edge → stable after soak
- README updated with snap install xboing instructions

### Out (separate bead, not now)
- Flatpak / AppImage / AUR / RPM
- CI auto-build of snap on tag (manual first release; CI integration later)

## Phases
1. Land the path resolver + SDL_GetPrefPath migration on master via a normal PR; verify .deb still works
2. Write and validate snapcraft.yaml locally (snapcraft pack)
3. snapcraft register xboing (one-time, names are first-come)
4. Launchpad remote-build for amd64 + arm64
5. Upload + release to edge channel
6. Verify install + run on clean Ubuntu (amd64 minimum; arm64 if hardware available)
7. Soak on edge — duration TBD by maintainer
8. Promote to stable

## Technical notes
- Confinement: strict (NOT classic) — strict + standard plugs gives automated review
- Architectures: amd64 + arm64 only. Skip i386, armhf, ppc64el, s390x — not worth the test surface
- Audio: audio-playback plug (not the deprecated pulseaudio plug)
- Joystick: joystick plug if we want gamepad; otherwise drop
- The snap and .deb both build with -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr; snap installs that tree to $SNAP/usr/share/xboing/, .deb installs it to /usr/share/xboing/. Same relative layout, different roots, $SNAP is the only thing that differs at runtime.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Path resolver implemented and used by all asset-loading call sites
- [ ] HIGH_SCORE_FILE migrated to SDL_GetPrefPath; old path no longer referenced
- [ ] .deb still builds clean and game runs from desktop launcher post-migration (regression check)
- [ ] snapcraft.yaml committed; builds clean via snapcraft remote-build --target-arch=amd64,arm64
- [ ] xboing snap installable via snap install xboing --edge on clean Ubuntu 22.04 and 24.04
- [ ] Game launches from desktop launcher (not just terminal) under confinement
- [ ] Audio + input + rendering all work under strict confinement
- [ ] High scores persist across snap refreshes
- [ ] Store listing populated with description, screenshots, icon
- [ ] Soak window completed on edge with no blocker bugs
- [ ] Promoted to stable channel
- [ ] README updated with snap install xboing instructions

## References
- SDL_GetPrefPath: https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL2/SDL_GetPrefPath
- Snap environment variables: https://snapcraft.io/docs/environment-variables
- XDG Base Directory Spec: https://specifications.freedesktop.org/basedir/latest/
- Why not SDL_GetBasePath for FHS-installed apps: https://github.com/bazz1tv/snestracker/issues/60

title: Gun firing not gated by powerup — K key fires bullets without gun block pickup
description:
The K-key gun firing logic does not correctly gate on the SPECIAL_FAST_GUN flag — bug is bidirectional.

## Symptoms

- 2026-03-23 (original report): K fires bullets without picking up a gun block — gating fails to reject when fastGun is off.
- 2026-05-01 (dogfood by maintainer): After picking up a gun block 3 times in level 1, K still does not fire — gating fails to accept when fastGun is on.

Both directions point to a flow bug in the chain: gun-block pickup → SPECIAL_FAST_GUN flag → K-key handler.

## Investigate

Read-set:
- original/gun.c (legacy gun fire logic)
- src/gun_system.c (current implementation)
- src/keys_system.c or src/game_input.c (K-key handler)
- src/special_system.c (SPECIAL_FAST_GUN flag flow + special_system_set/is_active)
- include/gun_system.h, include/special_system.h
- src/game_callbacks.c (callback wiring between systems)

## Resolution

K fires only when SPECIAL_FAST_GUN is active AND the gun was actually picked up; correctly toggles on/off per pickup and per level cycle.

title: [bug] Presents screen 'XBOING' letters use wrong fixed 80px stride instead of variable widths
description:
Audit pass 1 (m-2026-05-02-017) finding P10.

## Original
original/presents.c:319-354 — letters start at x=40, y=220, each letter at x += 10 + dists[i] where dists[] = {71, 73, 83, 41, 85, 88}. Variable per-letter widths to handle XBOING's varying glyph dimensions.

src/game_render_ui.c — add a const int letter_widths[] = {71, 73, 83, 41, 85, 88}; accumulate x = PLAY_AREA_X + 40, then per letter advance x += 10 + letter_widths[i]. Change y from 250 to 220 to match original (verify against PLAY_AREA_Y offset).

## Original
original/blocks.c:1754-1756 — renders roamer[slide] (5 frames: neutral + 4 directional L/R/U/D). bonusSlide is set randomly (0-4) from rand() % 5 via HandlePendingAnimations. Block visually 'looks around'.

title: Research: Reverse special persistence semantics across levels and ball deaths
description:
Open question — not yet confirmed bug. Maintainer observed in dogfood (2026-05-01) that Reverse remained active when transitioning between levels in level 1. Need to determine canonical original behavior before deciding if this is a bug or aligned-with-design.

## Questions to answer

1. **Across levels**: When the player completes a level and starts the next, does Reverse persist or clear in the original 1996 game?
2. **Across ball deaths / lives**: When the player loses a ball and respawns, does Reverse persist or clear?
3. **Modern alignment**: Whatever the canonical answer is, does the modern code align?

## Research approach

Read original/main.c (handleGameMode level transition + ball death paths), original/special.c (TurnSpecialsOff), original/paddle.c (reverseOn lifecycle). Note that include/special_system.h:128-130 documents special_system_turn_off as 'matches legacy TurnSpecialsOff behavior — clears all specials except saving on level transition' — this is a CLAIM about legacy behavior; verify.

Then trace modern wiring: is special_system_turn_off called at level transitions and/or ball deaths? Does it cascade to paddle_system to clear reverse_on (since Reverse is owned by paddle_system, not special_system)?

## Resolution paths

- Legacy clears across both → modern bug if it doesn't clear; minimal fix scope named
- Legacy persists across both → modern is correct; close with reason
- Legacy clears across one but not the other → match legacy in modern; document the asymmetry
